chunks.text:
Section heading: Online Safety Bill

**Written evidence submitted to the Public Bill Committee by the Office of the City Remembrancer, on behalf of the City of London Corporation and City of London Police (OSB65)**
chunks.display_text:
Section heading: Online Safety Bill

**Written evidence submitted to the Public Bill Committee by the Office of the City Remembrancer, on behalf of the City of London Corporation and City of London Police (OSB65)**

chunks.text:
Section heading: ONLINE SAFETY BILL

1.The City of London Corporation and City of London Police welcome the opportunity to provide written evidence to the Public Bill Committee and wish to record their support for the Bill’s provisions on fraud, which will play a key role in tackling the growing issue of online scams.

***The role of the City of London Police***

2.The City of London Police lead for the National Police Chiefs’ Council on economic and cybercrime and are the National Lead Force for Fraud. The City of London Police operates Action Fraud and the National Fraud Intelligence Bureau, which is the national reporting and recording centre for fraud and financially motivated cyber-crime. James Thomson CC, Chair of the City of London Police Authority Board, is also Deputy Lead for Economic and Cyber Crime and the only politically independent Board member of the Association of Police and Crime Commissioners. Angela McLaren joined the City of London Police in November 2020 as Assistant Commissioner and succeeded as Commissioner in January 2022. She is responsible for the NPCC Economic and Cyber Crime Portfolio.

3.As National Lead Force for Fraud, the City of London Police investigates serious, complex and cross-border fraud. It provides training and continuous professional development for the police and private sector workforce through its Economic Crime Academy.

***The growing issue of online fraud***

4.Compared with 2019 average reporting levels, there has been a significant increase in frauds facilitated through online channels. This includes:
chunks.display_text:
Section heading: ONLINE SAFETY BILL

1.The City of London Corporation and City of London Police welcome the opportunity to provide written evidence to the Public Bill Committee and wish to record their support for the Bill’s provisions on fraud, which will play a key role in tackling the growing issue of online scams.

***The role of the City of London Police***

2.The City of London Police lead for the National Police Chiefs’ Council on economic and cybercrime and are the National Lead Force for Fraud. The City of London Police operates Action Fraud and the National Fraud Intelligence Bureau, which is the national reporting and recording centre for fraud and financially motivated cyber-crime. James Thomson CC, Chair of the City of London Police Authority Board, is also Deputy Lead for Economic and Cyber Crime and the only politically independent Board member of the Association of Police and Crime Commissioners. Angela McLaren joined the City of London Police in November 2020 as Assistant Commissioner and succeeded as Commissioner in January 2022. She is responsible for the NPCC Economic and Cyber Crime Portfolio.

3.As National Lead Force for Fraud, the City of London Police investigates serious, complex and cross-border fraud. It provides training and continuous professional development for the police and private sector workforce through its Economic Crime Academy.

***The growing issue of online fraud***

4.Compared with 2019 average reporting levels, there has been a significant increase in frauds facilitated through online channels. This includes:

chunks.text: serious, complex and cross-border fraud. It provides training and continuous professional development for the police and private sector workforce through its Economic Crime Academy. ***The growing issue of online fraud*** 4.Compared with 2019 average reporting levels, there has been a significant increase in frauds facilitated through online channels. This includes: o **Online shopping and auction fraud** (43% increase) o **Romance fraud** (15% increase) o **Investment fraud** (16%increase) 5.There has been a notable increase in the number of investment fraud victims who have been contacted via social media. Social media companies and internet service providers often remove websites and accounts promoting investment frauds too late to prevent the public from investing. 6.Online shopping and auction fraud are often promoted through social networking sites and account for around a quarter of all fraud reported to Action Fraud. Romance fraud continues to increase year on year. The majority of frauds are perpetrated online. 7.While the City of London Police are working hard with partners to coordinate pursing individuals responsible and providing protect advice to the public, the scale and reach of this threat requires a whole system approach to tackle it effectively. This must include an obligation on the private sector to continue to play its part. 8.This Bill presents an opportunity to address a wide range of online enablers to protect the public, reducing the accessibility that criminals currently freely exploit to spread significant harm online. ***The Bill’s fraud provisions***
chunks.display_text: o **Online shopping and auction fraud** (43% increase) o **Romance fraud** (15% increase) o **Investment fraud** (16%increase) 5.There has been a notable increase in the number of investment fraud victims who have been contacted via social media. Social media companies and internet service providers often remove websites and accounts promoting investment frauds too late to prevent the public from investing. 6.Online shopping and auction fraud are often promoted through social networking sites and account for around a quarter of all fraud reported to Action Fraud. Romance fraud continues to increase year on year. The majority of frauds are perpetrated online. 7.While the City of London Police are working hard with partners to coordinate pursing individuals responsible and providing protect advice to the public, the scale and reach of this threat requires a whole system approach to tackle it effectively. This must include an obligation on the private sector to continue to play its part. 8.This Bill presents an opportunity to address a wide range of online enablers to protect the public, reducing the accessibility that criminals currently freely exploit to spread significant harm online. ***The Bill’s fraud provisions***

chunks.text: effectively. This must include an obligation on the private sector to continue to play its part. 8.This Bill presents an opportunity to address a wide range of online enablers to protect the public, reducing the accessibility that criminals currently freely exploit to spread significant harm online. ***The Bill’s fraud provisions*** 9.The City of London Police welcome the change in view regarding categorising fraud as a priority harm. There is a growing realisation of the spread and depth of the harm caused by fraud now accounting for over a third of all crime. During 2020/21, 16% of crime reports to Action Fraud featured at least one social media and communication platform with attributed losses in excess of £165 million. This compared to 12% in the previous year. 10.The degree of harm for individuals can be significant. Last year Action Fraud call handlers supported around 200 individuals expressing intent of suicide or other self-harm requiring an immediate police response. Far from being a victimless crime, fraud is extremely harmful and the impact on victims can be long lasting, often with an unwarranted sense of complicity with the criminals as they may have given them access to their life savings. 11.Inclusion of fraud as a priority harm in the Bill provides much needed duty for online service providers to take proactive steps to identify and stop malicious content linked to fraud and protect users of their services. 12.The inclusion of paid-for advertising within the scope of the Bill is also welcomed. Use of online advertisements helps propagate a wide range of frauds including online shopping, holiday fraud and investment fraud, which often have life-changing consequences for victims. Without the measures in the Bill, social media platforms could make considerable returns on illegitimate adverts being placed on their sites, essentially profiting from criminal activity.
chunks.display_text: 9.The City of London Police welcome the change in view regarding categorising fraud as a priority harm. There is a growing realisation of the spread and depth of the harm caused by fraud now accounting for over a third of all crime. During 2020/21, 16% of crime reports to Action Fraud featured at least one social media and communication platform with attributed losses in excess of £165 million. This compared to 12% in the previous year. 10.The degree of harm for individuals can be significant. Last year Action Fraud call handlers supported around 200 individuals expressing intent of suicide or other self-harm requiring an immediate police response. Far from being a victimless crime, fraud is extremely harmful and the impact on victims can be long lasting, often with an unwarranted sense of complicity with the criminals as they may have given them access to their life savings. 11.Inclusion of fraud as a priority harm in the Bill provides much needed duty for online service providers to take proactive steps to identify and stop malicious content linked to fraud and protect users of their services. 12.The inclusion of paid-for advertising within the scope of the Bill is also welcomed. Use of online advertisements helps propagate a wide range of frauds including online shopping, holiday fraud and investment fraud, which often have life-changing consequences for victims. Without the measures in the Bill, social media platforms could make considerable returns on illegitimate adverts being placed on their sites, essentially profiting from criminal activity.

chunks.text: online advertisements helps propagate a wide range of frauds including online shopping, holiday fraud and investment fraud, which often have life-changing consequences for victims. Without the measures in the Bill, social media platforms could make considerable returns on illegitimate adverts being placed on their sites, essentially profiting from criminal activity. 13.The inclusion of user-generated fraud within the scope of the Bill will make a real difference in tackling scams such as fake investment opportunities and romance fraud. Not only will this benefit law enforcement agencies in their struggle against these scams, but the Bill will have a huge benefit on the thousands of victims of these emotionally impacting crimes. However, it is unlikely that this Bill will achieve the aim of making the UK the safest place to be online on its own. While many of the measures within this Bill are to be welcomed, the threat to individuals online is extremely broad and extends beyond the scope of this Bill. Within the area of online fraud, this Bill does nothing to tackle fraud via emails or cloned websites. 14.The City of London Police also welcome the Bill’s wider provisions, beyond those tackling fraud, to protect individuals from serious harm online. ***Office of the City Remembrancer*** ***June 2022***
chunks.display_text: 13.The inclusion of user-generated fraud within the scope of the Bill will make a real difference in tackling scams such as fake investment opportunities and romance fraud. Not only will this benefit law enforcement agencies in their struggle against these scams, but the Bill will have a huge benefit on the thousands of victims of these emotionally impacting crimes. However, it is unlikely that this Bill will achieve the aim of making the UK the safest place to be online on its own. While many of the measures within this Bill are to be welcomed, the threat to individuals online is extremely broad and extends beyond the scope of this Bill. Within the area of online fraud, this Bill does nothing to tackle fraud via emails or cloned websites. 14.The City of London Police also welcome the Bill’s wider provisions, beyond those tackling fraud, to protect individuals from serious harm online. ***Office of the City Remembrancer*** ***June 2022***
